Operation 'VerifySenderToken' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'Verify Sender Request' is vague and uses imprecise terminology ('Request' vs 'Identity'). Description lacks critical details about the operation's effects (status change to verified, authorization to send), expected inputs (token validation requirements), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (state change in the system). The improved version clarifies the action (completing verification), explains the token's role, specifies the outcome (sender becomes authorized), and helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation in the sender verification workflow.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to verify a sender requests.**

The token is generated by SendGrid and included in a verification email delivered to the address that's pending verification.
data.summary_suggestion: Verify a sender identity using token
data.description_suggestion: Completes the sender identity verification process by validating the verification token sent to the pending sender's email address. The token is generated by SendGrid and included in the verification email. Upon successful verification, the sender identity status is updated to verified and becomes authorized to send email. Returns the verified sender identity details. Requires a valid, unexpired token from the verification email.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Verify Sender Request' is vague and uses imprecise terminology ('Request' vs 'Identity'). Description lacks critical details about the operation's effects (status change to verified, authorization to send), expected inputs (token validation requirements), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (state change in the system). The improved version clarifies the action (completing verification), explains the token's role, specifies the outcome (sender becomes authorized), and helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation in the sender verification workflow.

message: Operation 'CreateVerifiedSender' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Create Verified Sender Request' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the action. Description lacks clarity about what is returned (response schema/object structure). Added specific details about the verification email trigger, unverified state, and return value to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to create a new Sender Identify**.

Upon successful submission of a `POST` request to this endpoint, an identity will be created, and a verification email will be sent to the address assigned to the `from_email` field. You must complete the verification process using the sent email to fully verify the sender.

If you need to resend the verification email, you can do so with the Resend Verified Sender Request, `/resend/{id}`, endpoint.

If you need to authenticate a domain rather than a Single Sender, see the [Domain Authentication API](https://docs.sendgrid.com/api-reference/domain-authentication/authenticate-a-domain).
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new Sender Identity and send verification email
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Sender Identity for your account. Requires a `from_email` address in the request body. Upon successful creation, a verification email is automatically sent to the specified email address. The Sender Identity remains unverified until the recipient completes the verification process via the sent email. Returns the created Sender Identity object with a 201 status code. Use the Resend Verified Sender endpoint to resend verification emails if needed.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Create Verified Sender Request' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the action. Description lacks clarity about what is returned (response schema/object structure). Added specific details about the verification email trigger, unverified state, and return value to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'UpdateVerifiedSender' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity about what is returned and doesn't explicitly state the side effect (which fields are modified vs. preserved). Added explicit return value information and clarified the partial update behavior for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to update an existing Sender Identity**.

Pass the `id` assigned to a Sender Identity to this endpoint as a path parameter. Include any fields you wish to update in the request body in JSON format.

You can retrieve the IDs associated with Sender Identities by passing a `GET` request to the Get All Verified Senders endpoint, `/verified_senders`.

**Note:** Unlike a `PUT` request, `PATCH` allows you to update only the fields you wish to edit. Fields that are not passed as part of a request will remain unaltered.
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing Sender Identity with the specified ID. Pass the Sender Identity ID as a path parameter and include the fields to update in the JSON request body. Supports partial updates—only provided fields are modified, while unspecified fields remain unchanged. Returns the updated Sender Identity object with a 200 status code. Retrieve Sender Identity IDs using the List Verified Senders endpoint.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity about what is returned and doesn't explicitly state the side effect (which fields are modified vs. preserved). Added explicit return value information and clarified the partial update behavior for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'DeleteVerifiedSender' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks information about the permanent nature of deletion and the expected response status code. Added explicit warning about irreversibility and clarified the 204 response to help AI agents understand the side effects and expected behavior.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to delete a Sender Identity**.

Pass the `id` assigned to a Sender Identity to this endpoint to delete the Sender Identity from your account.

You can retrieve the IDs associated with Sender Identities using the "Get All Verified Senders" endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a Sender Identity from your account. Pass the Sender Identity ID as a path parameter. This action cannot be undone—the Sender Identity will no longer be available for sending email. Returns a 204 No Content status on successful deletion. Retrieve Sender Identity IDs using the List Verified Senders endpoint.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks information about the permanent nature of deletion and the expected response status code. Added explicit warning about irreversibility and clarified the 204 response to help AI agents understand the side effects and expected behavior.

message: Operation 'ListVerifiedSenderDomain' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, inconsistent_terminology. Original summary 'Domain Warn List' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the operation's purpose. Terminology should be consistent with API context (use 'Sender Identity' consistently). Description could better explain the practical implications of hard vs. soft failures and the operation's purpose for AI agents deciding when to use it.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint returns a list of domains known to implement DMARC and categorizes them by failure type — hard failure or soft failure**.

Domains listed as hard failures will not deliver mail when used as a [Sender Identity](https://sendgrid.com/docs/for-developers/sending-email/sender-identity/) due to the domain's DMARC policy settings.

For example, using a `yahoo.com` email address as a Sender Identity will likely result in the rejection of your mail. For more information about DMARC, see [Everything about DMARC](https://sendgrid.com/docs/ui/sending-email/dmarc/).
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve domains with DMARC implementation warnings
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of domains known to implement DMARC policies, categorized by failure type: hard failures and soft failures. Hard failure domains will reject mail when used as Sender Identities due to strict DMARC policy enforcement (e.g., yahoo.com). Soft failure domains may have delivery issues depending on DMARC configuration. Use this endpoint to identify problematic domains before creating Sender Identities. Returns a list of domains with their failure classifications.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Domain Warn List' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the operation's purpose. Terminology should be consistent with API context (use 'Sender Identity' consistently). Description could better explain the practical implications of hard vs. soft failures and the operation's purpose for AI agents deciding when to use it.

message: Operation 'ResendVerifiedSender' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary 'Resend Verified Sender Request' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the action. Description lacks information about the response and doesn't clarify that the Sender Identity remains unverified until the new email is acted upon. Added explicit return value and clarified the side effect (email is resent but verification status unchanged).

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to resend a verification email to a specified Sender Identity**.

Passing the `id` assigned to a Sender Identity to this endpoint will resend a verification email to the `from_address` associated with the Sender Identity. This can be useful if someone loses their verification email or needs to have it resent for any other reason.

You can retrieve the IDs associated with Sender Identities by passing a "Get All Verified Senders" endpoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Resend verification email to Sender Identity
data.description_suggestion: Resends a verification email to the address associated with a specified Sender Identity. Pass the Sender Identity ID as a path parameter. The verification email will be sent to the `from_email` address of the Sender Identity. Useful when the original verification email is lost or needs to be resent. Returns the Sender Identity object with a 200 status code. Retrieve Sender Identity IDs using the List Verified Senders endpoint.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Resend Verified Sender Request' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the action. Description lacks information about the response and doesn't clarify that the Sender Identity remains unverified until the new email is acted upon. Added explicit return value and clarified the side effect (email is resent but verification status unchanged).

message: Operation 'ListVerifiedSenderStepsCompleted' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, unclear_purpose, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Completed Steps' is too vague and doesn't indicate what is being checked. Description is unclear about the specific return format and fields. Added clarity about the boolean response fields, their meanings, and the practical use case for AI agents to understand when to call this endpoint.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to determine which of SendGrid's verification processes have been completed for an account**.

This endpoint returns boolean values, `true` and `false`, for [Domain Authentication](https://sendgrid.com/docs/for-developers/sending-email/sender-identity/#domain-authentication), `domain_verified`, and [Single Sender Verification](https://sendgrid.com/docs/for-developers/sending-email/sender-identity/#single-sender-verification), `sender_verified`, for the account.

An account may have one, both, or neither verification steps completed. If you need to authenticate a domain rather than a Single Sender, see the "Authenticate a domain" endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the verification completion status for your account. Returns a response object containing two boolean fields: `domain_verified` (indicates if Domain Authentication is complete) and `sender_verified` (indicates if Single Sender Verification is complete). An account may have one, both, or neither verification steps completed. Use this endpoint to determine which verification processes are required before sending email. Returns a 200 status with the verification status object.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Completed Steps' is too vague and doesn't indicate what is being checked. Description is unclear about the specific return format and fields. Added clarity about the boolean response fields, their meanings, and the practical use case for AI agents to understand when to call this endpoint.
